title: dendrophthoe philippensis
intro: Dendrophthoe philippensis, also known as the woolly mistletoe or dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, obtaining its nutrients by connecting to the host's vascular tissues. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Dendrophthoe philippensis is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, or applied topically in the form of creams and ointments.
